title: How al -Mulabbid Came to Be Killed
description: It is said that when al-Mulabbid226 had bested IHumayd b. Qahtabah, and IIumayd, in turn, had taken refuge from alMulabbid, Abu Ja`far sent against him `Abd al-`Aziz b. `Abd alRabman,227 the brother of `Abd al-Jabbar b. `Abd al-Rahman,22s in conjunction with Ziyad b. Mushkan. Al-Mulabbid waylaid him [123] with ioo cavalry in such a way that when `Abd al-`Aziz met up with him, those lying in ambush came forth and routed him, killing the greater part of his confederates. At this Abu Ja`far dispatched against him Khazim b.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 28
